issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides an outline-offset. Without a non-zero outline, box-shadow, or border, there is no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 314: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly removes the outline for elements that do not match :focus-visible. If a browser fails to support :focus-visible or incorrectly categorizes an interaction, the focus indicator is lost entirely.
issuesFound.fix: Line 321: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible (forced-colors)
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the focus indicator is set to outline: none. In high-contrast mode, box-shadows are often disabled, making a visible outline mandatory for navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 327: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button component explicitly removes the outline on focus-visible without providing a replacement (like a box-shadow or border) within the same rule block.
issuesFound.fix: Line 514: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link removes the focus outline. Keyboard users will not be able to visually track their position when navigating to the site home link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 585: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle removes the focus indicator. This prevents keyboard users on mobile-width viewports from identifying the active element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 621: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links remove the outline and only provide an outline-offset. Since the outline width is zero by default (due to the global reset), no indicator appears.
issuesFound.fix: Line 685: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes the outline on focus. While it provides a box-shadow, box-shadows are often stripped in High Contrast Mode, leaving the element without an indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 718: #search-input:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input explicitly removes the outline for focus-visible states, which are specifically intended for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 725: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submission button removes the focus indicator, making it impossible for keyboard users to verify they have reached the submit action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 748: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the focus outline, failing to provide a visible indicator for keyboard-only users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 771: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.explanation: The "Learn more" links in feature cards remove the focus outline, which is a failure of SC 2.4.7 Focus Visible.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus

issuesFound.explanation: Form controls explicitly remove the focus-visible outline, which is the primary indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1639: .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon height/width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Icon buttons are explicitly sized at 20px by 20px, making them difficult to activate for users with motor impairments or those using touch screens.

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"]

issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px target size.

issuesFound.title: visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class resets width/height to auto and overflow to visible. This fails to visually hide the content if the element contains text, and the lack of clipping means it may interfere with the visual layout.
issuesFound.fix: Line 346: width: 1px; Line 347: height: 1px; Line 350: overflow: hidden; Line 351: clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); clip-path: inset(50%); position: absolute;

issuesFound.title: negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: Setting word-spacing to -0.2em crowds text together, making it difficult for users with low vision or dyslexia to distinguish individual words.

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: Negative letter-spacing (-0.05em) reduces the legibility of body text by causing characters to overlap or appear too close.
issuesFound.fix: Line 219: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: The hero heading uses --letter-spacing-tighter (-0.05em), which significantly degrades readability for large text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 831: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1445: `_open = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` Line 1461: `_open = false; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function in the `productFilter` module updates the view mode but does not update the `aria-pressed` state on the buttons. This violates SC 4.1.2 as the state of the toggle is not programmatically determinable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1841: `setAttr(b, 'aria-pressed', String(b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === mode));`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module adds a visual error class and shows an error message but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field. Screen readers will not announce the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2515: `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module clears error messages for valid fields but does not reset the `aria-invalid` attribute to "false". The field may continue to be announced as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2510: `removeClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function does not initialise the `aria-expanded` attribute on the hamburger trigger. Interactive elements that control visibility must have their initial state defined for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1515: `_trigger.addEventListener('click', _onTriggerClick); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `stickyHeader` module hides and shows the header based on scroll direction using CSS classes but does not update `aria-hidden`. This can result in keyboard focus being trapped in a visually hidden header.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1745: `addClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden); setAttr(_header, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` Line 1750: `removeClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden); setAttr(_header, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user toggles between 'grid' and 'list' view modes in the `productFilter` module, the change is visual only. There is no live-region announcement to inform screen reader users that the layout has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1841: `_productFilterState.viewMode = mode; liveRegion.announce(`View changed to ${mode} layout`);`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the expanded/collapsed state of the navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1670: In the open function, add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); after _open = true;
Line 1674: In the close function, add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); after _open = false;

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions toggle the visibility of dropdown panels but do not update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know whether the dropdown is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1760: In _openInstance, add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); after inst.open = true;
Line 1764: In _closeInstance, add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); after inst.open = false;

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open and close functions toggle the visibility of FAQ answer panels but do not update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be aware of the expanded/collapsed state of each FAQ item.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2240: In the open function, add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); after item.open = true;
Line 2244: In the close function, add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); after item.open = false;

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._onToggleClick function toggles between monthly and annual billing but does not update the aria-pressed state on the toggle button. Screen reader users will not know which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1500: In _onToggleClick, add setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, next === 'year'); after const next = _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'month' : 'year';

issuesFound.title: Product filter updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visible product cards and count badge but does not announce these changes via an aria-live region. Screen reader users will not be informed when filter results change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1340: After the emit call, add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : `Showing ${_productFilterState.activeCategory} products`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function marks invalid fields with a CSS class but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen reader users will not be alerted to the invalid state of form fields.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2600: In _renderResult, when result.valid is false, add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true); before addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);
Line 2601: When result.valid is true, add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false); before addClass(field, CSS.fieldValid);

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the navigation toggle button when the drawer is closed on page load. Screen reader users may not know the initial state of the navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1690: In init, after _trigger is assigned, add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button when the dropdown is closed on page load. Screen reader users may not know the initial state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1780: In register, after setting aria-controls, add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden mobile navigation drawer not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function sets the hidden attribute on the drawer but does not set aria-hidden="true". This may cause screen readers to still announce the drawer content when it is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1674: In close, add setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true); after _open = false;

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden dropdown panel not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance function sets the hidden attribute on the panel but does not set aria-hidden="true". This may cause screen readers to still announce the panel content when it is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1764: In _closeInstance, add setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, true); after inst.open = false;

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden FAQ answer panel not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close function sets the hidden attribute on the panel but does not set aria-hidden="true". This may cause screen readers to still announce the answer content when it is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2244: In close, add setAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, true); after item.open = false;

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates price displays and savings messages but does not announce the change via an aria-live region. Screen reader users may not be aware that the billing period has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1480: In setPeriod, after _updateSavingsMessage(period), add liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open and close functions toggle the visibility of FAQ answer panels but do not announce the state change via an aria-live region. Screen reader users may not know when an FAQ item expands or collapses.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2240: In open, after emit, add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened ? STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question) : `${item.question} expanded`);
Line 2244: In close, after emit, add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed ? STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question) : `${item.question} collapsed`);

FAQ accordion open/close not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer's open and close functions do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the expanded/collapsed state of the navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1750: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`. Line 1768: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded set initially. Screen reader users will not know the initial state of the navigation drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1799: after `_initialised = true;` add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu triggers do not have aria-expanded set to false on initialization. Screen reader users will not know the initial collapsed state of dropdown panels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1963: in the `register` function, after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, the updated prices are not announced to screen readers. Users with visual impairments will not know that the price display has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2942: in `setPeriod`, after `_syncToggleState(period);` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices updated');`.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validation function marks fields with error classes but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers will not announce the invalid state of fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4074: in `_renderResult`, inside the else block (when result is invalid), add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`. In the valid block, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel visibility changes without aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel is hidden with the hidden attribute but aria-hidden is not set. When hidden via CSS, screen readers may still access the content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1896: in `_openInstance`, after `panel.removeAttribute('hidden');` add `panel.removeAttribute(ARIA.HIDDEN);`. Line 1906: in `_closeInstance`, after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` add `panel.setAttribute(ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer visibility changes without aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is hidden with CSS classes but aria-hidden is not updated. Screen readers may still access the drawer content when it is visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1754: in `open`, after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` add `_drawer.removeAttribute(ARIA.HIDDEN);`. Line 1772: in `close`, after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` add `_drawer.setAttribute(ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav lacks focus trap when drawer is open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, keyboard focus is not trapped inside it. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer, causing loss of context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1750: in `open`, after `_lockScroll();` add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer); _trap.activate();`. Line 1768: in `close`, after `_unlockScroll();` add `if (_trap) _trap.deactivate();`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu lacks focus trap when panel is open
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel opens, keyboard focus is not trapped inside it. Keyboard users can tab out of the panel, breaking the interaction model.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1896: in `_openInstance`, after `panel.removeAttribute('hidden');` add `inst.trap = createFocusTrap(panel); inst.trap.activate();`. Line 1906: in `_closeInstance`, after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` add `if (inst.trap) inst.trap.deactivate();`. Also, add a `trap` property to the `inst` object in the `register` function.

fpTitles: mobileNav drawer visibility changes without aria-hidden
dropdownMenu lacks focus trap when panel is open

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.toggle function changes the visibility of the navigation drawer but does not update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the expanded/collapsed state of the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions, set aria-expanded on the trigger button. For example, in the open function add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); and in the close function add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the product grid but does not announce the filter result to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may not know that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: After applying filters, announce the result count via liveRegion.announce. For example: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator.validate function marks fields as invalid but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen reader users may not be alerted to the error.
issuesFound.fix: In the _renderResult function of inlineValidator, when the result is invalid, set aria-invalid="true" on the field. For example: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button when the drawer is closed on page load. Screen reader users may not know the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init, after setting up the trigger and drawer, set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. For example: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function sets aria-controls on the trigger only if it doesn't already have it, but the panel may not have an id. This can break the association between the trigger and the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the panel has an id and set aria-controls on the trigger. For example: const panelId = panel.id || srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel'); setAttr(trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, panelId);

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and close functions change the navigation drawer visibility but do not announce the state change via an aria-live region. Screen reader users may not be aware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: In the open and close functions, use liveRegion.announce to announce the state change. For example: liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu opened');

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel play button (_playBtn) has a click event listener but no keydown handler for Enter and Space. If the element is not a native button, it will not be keyboard accessible.
issuesFound.fix: Add a keydown event listener to the play button that triggers the togglePlay function on Enter and Space. For example: _playBtn.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); togglePlay(); } });

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator.validate function sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields but does not clear it (set to false or remove) when the field becomes valid. This may cause screen readers to incorrectly announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In the _renderResult function, when the result is valid, set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute. For example: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

fpTitles: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified when the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1146: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` (inside open), Line 1153: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` (inside close)

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function reveals the navigation drawer, but there is no corresponding write to an aria-live region to announce that the menu has opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1146: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions in the dropdown menu controller change the visibility of the dropdown panel but never set or clear `aria-expanded` on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the panel’s state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hide does not set aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `_close` method of the mobile navigation controller removes CSS classes and scroll‑locks the body but never adds `aria-hidden="true"` to the drawer element, so the hidden drawer remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In `_close`, after hiding the drawer, call `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `_open`, remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle buttons (`data-period` buttons) are used as a two‑state selector (monthly/annual) but never update `aria-pressed`, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which period is active.
issuesFound.fix: In `setPeriod`, after updating the visual state, set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', btn.getAttribute('data-period') === period ? 'true' : 'false')` for each period button.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates prices on the cards but does not provide an audible announcement; users of assistive technology receive no feedback that the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: After updating all plan cards in `setPeriod`, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Pricing updated for selected billing period')`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on error fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` marks fields as invalid visually but only calls `srUtils.markInvalid`, which adds `aria-describedby` without setting `aria-invalid="true"`. Assistive technologies therefore do not know the field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In `srUtils.markInvalid`, add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true')`; in `srUtils.markValid`, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Search input (combobox) missing aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The site‑search input is given `role="combobox"` and `aria-controls`, but never updates `aria-expanded` to reflect whether the suggestion list is open, leaving screen‑reader users without state information.
issuesFound.fix: In `siteSearch._open` set `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_close` set it to `'false'`.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestions list does not set aria‑expanded on the input (duplicate of Issue 7)
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 7 – the live listbox is shown/hidden but the associated combobox input never reflects the expanded state via `aria-expanded`.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 7 – update `aria-expanded` whenever the listbox is shown or hidden.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open/close does not set aria-controls on trigger when missing
issuesFound.explanation: Although the `register` function adds `aria-controls` if absent, some dropdowns are created dynamically elsewhere without this attribute, resulting in broken references.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure every dropdown trigger receives `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId)` where `panelId` is the ID of the associated panel.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates in pricing module lack dedicated live‑region announcements (duplicate of Issue 5 but separate function)
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing.setPeriod` function updates price text but does not write to a dedicated live‑region element; the generic status region used elsewhere is not updated, so the change is not announced.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden live‑region element (e.g., `
`) and set its `textContent` to a concise announcement each time `setPeriod` runs.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The functions `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` in the dropdownMenu module toggle visibility of panels but never set or update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger buttons, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: In both `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the faqAccordion module change the visibility of FAQ panels but never modify the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger buttons, breaking the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `close(item)` call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria-expanded on load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are given `aria-haspopup` but lack an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, violating the ARIA disclosure pattern on page load.
issuesFound.fix: During dropdownMenu initialization, set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` for each trigger.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-expanded on load
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons receive `aria-controls` but are not initialized with `aria-expanded="false"`, leaving their collapsed state ambiguous to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqRegistry.parse`, after setting `aria-controls`, also call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Search input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input receives `aria-controls` and other ARIA attributes but is never given an initial `aria-expanded="false"` value, breaking the combobox pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `suggestionListRenderer.mount`, after setting other ARIA attributes, add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu hide does not set aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is hidden (`_closeInstance`), the code only removes the `hidden` attribute but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the panel, so the element remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In `_closeInstance`, after hiding the panel, add `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `_openInstance`, remove the attribute with `inst.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel hide does not set aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function in faqAccordion hides the answer panel with `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`, leaving hidden content perceivable by AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `close(item)`, after setting `hidden`, also call `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `open(item)`, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Site search suggestion list hide does not set aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: `suggestionListRenderer.hide()` only adds the `hidden` attribute to the listbox without setting `aria-hidden="true"`, so hidden suggestions remain in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In `hide()`, after `setAttribute('hidden', '')`, also call `setAttr(_listEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `show()`, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Form fields validation does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` validates fields and displays error messages but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid inputs, preventing AT from detecting errors.
issuesFound.fix: In `_renderResult`, when `result.valid` is false, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; when valid, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing period buttons (`_periodBtns`) are toggled with CSS classes but never receive `aria-pressed` updates, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which period is active.
issuesFound.fix: In `_syncToggleState`, after toggling the active class, also call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', btn === activeBtn ? 'true' : 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Carousel play/pause button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel play/pause control toggles a CSS class but never updates `aria-pressed`, leaving its state ambiguous to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `togglePlay`, after changing play state, call `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', _playing ? 'true' : 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge` changes the numeric badge but does not announce the new count to a live region, so users of assistive tech are unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the badge text, set `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products shown`)` or add a hidden live region that receives the count.

issuesFound.title: Active filter chips render does not announce changes
issuesFound.explanation: `activeFilterChips.render` updates the chip list visually but does not provide an ARIA live announcement, so AT users cannot know filters were added or removed.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering, call `liveRegion.announce(`${action} filter ${label}`)` where `action` is “added” or “removed”.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown opens or closes, the code emits custom events but does not announce the state change via an ARIA live region, leaving screen‑reader users uninformed.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance`, after toggling visibility, call `liveRegion.announce(`${btn.textContent.trim()} menu ${opened ? 'opened' : 'closed'}`)`.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a

issuesFound.fix: Line 1010: Add text-decoration: underline; on hover/focus, or add border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links have text-decoration: none and rely solely on color change on hover, with no non-colour visual differentiator. Users with colour vision deficiency may not distinguish links from text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1026: Add text-decoration: underline; on hover/focus, or add border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring, leaving keyboard users with no indication of focus. This affects all interactive elements that rely on the default focus style.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus-visible rule removes the default outline with outline: none and provides only outline-offset: 3px. Without a box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline, keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the visible focus indicator using outline: none with only outline-offset. This critical control becomes invisible to keyboard users when focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links ([id$="-submenu"] li a) have their focus outline removed with no visible replacement. The negative outline-offset (-3px) combined with outline: none renders focus completely invisible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search form submit button removes the focus outline without providing a box-shadow or border alternative, leaving keyboard users without focus feedback.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on FAQ buttons
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove focus outlines with only a negative outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on form checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: Custom checkbox inputs remove focus outlines without providing a visible alternative, making it impossible for keyboard users to see when checkboxes are focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove focus outlines with only outline-offset, leaving keyboard users without focus feedback.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on social links
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links in the footer remove focus outlines without replacement, preventing keyboard users from identifying the focused element.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on table wrapper
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes focus outlines without replacement. Keyboard users tabbing to this region receive no visual feedback.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on header logo link
issuesFound.explanation: The header logo link removes focus outlines with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The forced-colors media query removes focus outlines without providing a visible alternative. Users in Windows High Contrast mode lose all focus indication.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, creating touch targets smaller than the 24px minimum. This affects users with tremors or limited fine motor control.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: Custom checkboxes have width: 20px and height: 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes them difficult for users with motor disabilities to select accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on navigation toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle button has min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px, which meets the minimum 24px requirement but falls below the recommended 44px for optimal accessibility. Without sufficient surrounding spacing, users may accidentally activate adjacent elements.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. While it meets the minimum 24px requirement, the small size may cause difficulty for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, meaning it does not actually hide content visually. Screen-reader-only text will be visible on screen, breaking the layout and potentially exposing content meant only for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, causing spatial movement on interaction. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disables this animation, which can trigger vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform properties that creates spatial movement. The empty @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 901 fails to disable this animation, potentially causing issues for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on feature cards
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards transition on transform, creating movement on hover. The reduced-motion media query only sets transform: none on hover but does not remove the transition property, so animation still occurs.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on skip link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses transform in its transition for the translateY movement. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see this animation as it lacks an override.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em. Negative word-spacing reduces the space between words, making it difficult for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities to distinguish word boundaries.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on secondary text in features section
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p uses colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.2:1. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text and the 3:1 requirement for large text (18px).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on product description text
issuesFound.explanation: Product card paragraphs use #aaaaaa on #ffffff (2.2:1 contrast ratio), failing the 4.5:1 requirement for the 14px font size used.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on table "not included" indicators
issuesFound.explanation: Table cells indicating "Not included" or "Not available" use colour-neutral-300 (#cbd5e1) on a white or near-white background (#f8fafc), resulting in approximately 1.4:1 contrast ratio. This fails the 3:1 requirement for the 20px large text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links use colour-neutral-400 (#94a3b8) on colour-neutral-900 (#555555), resulting in approximately 2.8:1 contrast ratio. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for 14px text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer address text
issuesFound.explanation: The address text in the footer uses colour-neutral-500 (#64748b) on #555555 background, resulting in approximately 1.6:1 contrast ratio. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for 14px text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on input hints
issuesFound.explanation: Input hint text uses #aaaaaa on white background (2.2:1 ratio), failing the 4.5:1 requirement for the 12px font size.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on main navigation
issuesFound.explanation: Main navigation links (#nav-menu > li > a) have text-decoration: none (inherited from global a rule) and rely solely on colour changes for hover states. No underline, border-bottom, or font-weight change is provided as a non-colour visual differentiator.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on footer links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove underlines and provide only colour changes on hover. Without underlines or other non-colour indicators, users with colour vision deficiencies cannot distinguish links from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is opened and closed via the open() and close() functions, but neither updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the state change. Screen readers will not announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1898: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `_open = true;`
  Line 1916: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels but fails to set aria-expanded="true" or aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button (inst.trigger). This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2085: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside _openInstance after `inst.open = true;`
  Line 2103: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside _closeInstance after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion panel is toggled open and closed, but the combobox input (_inputEl) does not have its aria-expanded attribute updated to reflect the visibility of the suggestion listbox.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2674: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside _open()
  Line 2686: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside _close()

issuesFound.title: productFilter category tabs do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons indicate active state via CSS classes but do not set aria-pressed="true" on the active button or aria-pressed="false" on inactive buttons. This is required for toggle buttons to communicate their state to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as invalid, the function adds an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1425: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after the addDescribedBy call

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is marked valid, the function removes the error description but does not set aria-invalid="false" (or remove the attribute), leaving the field incorrectly flagged as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1444: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` (or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`) after clearing aria-describedby

issuesFound.title: mobileNav aria-expanded not initialized at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialized to "false" when the page loads and the menu is closed. Additionally, the trigger lacks aria-controls pointing to the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1982: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` and `if (_drawer) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu aria-expanded not initialized at page load
issuesFound.explanation: While the dropdown panel is hidden on initialization, the trigger button does not have aria-expanded explicitly set to "false" during registration.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2205: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after setting aria-controls

issuesFound.title: siteSearch aria-expanded not initialized at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is assigned role="combobox" but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" when the suggestion list is hidden on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2550: Add `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after setting aria-haspopup

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not setting aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile nav drawer is closed, it is hidden via CSS class removal but aria-hidden="true" is not set, leaving the drawer in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1920: Add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` inside close()
  Line 1900: Add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');` or `removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN);` inside open()

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel not setting aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel is hidden with the hidden attribute after a timeout, but aria-hidden="true" is not set, potentially leaving it accessible to screen readers during the transition.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2108: Add `setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` inside the setTimeout callback
  Line 2085: Add `setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');` or `removeAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN);` inside _openInstance

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion panel not setting aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ answer panel is collapsed and receives the hidden attribute via animateHeight callback, but aria-hidden="true" is not set on the panel.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3468: Add `setAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` inside the animateHeight onComplete callback
  Line 3432: Add `setAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');` or `removeAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN);` inside open()

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the page state significantly, but no announcement is made via the live region to inform screen reader users that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1902: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` inside open()
  Line 1926: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` inside close()

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menus opening and closing are not announced to screen readers via the live region, leaving users unaware that content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2091: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu opened');` inside _openInstance
  Line 2112: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu closed');` inside _closeInstance

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and product cards are shown/hidden, the result count is updated visually but not announced via a live region, so screen reader users do not know that filtering has completed or how many results are shown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3195: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) || 'Products filtered');` after _updateCountBadge

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing updates prices visually but does not announce the change or the new prices via the live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3080: Add `liveRegion.announce(_pricingState.period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion expand/collapse not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items changes visible content but does not announce the action or the question content via the live region.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3440: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question) || item.question + ' expanded');`
  Line 3470: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question) || item.question + ' collapsed');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a focus trap variable (_trap) but never initializes it with createFocusTrap(), and never activates it when the menu opens. Keyboard focus is not trapped within the open mobile menu, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1983: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` after querying _drawer and _trigger
  Line 1900: Add `_trap.activate();` inside open() function after _lockScroll()
  Line 1928: Keep `_trap.deactivate();` inside close() (already present)

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the id of the drawer element (_drawer), which is required for the expanded/collapsed relationship to be programmatically determined.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1983: Add `if (_drawer) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));` after ensuring _drawer has an id

fpTitles: dropdownMenu aria-expanded not initialized at page load
dropdownMenu panel not setting aria-hidden when closed
pricing period change not announced via live region
faqAccordion expand/collapse not announced via live region
mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels by manipulating CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but fails to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded or collapsed state of the dropdown menu to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in _openInstance(): After `inst.open = true;` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line in _closeInstance(): After `inst.open = false;` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function adds the open CSS class and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. While the close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", this mismatch means screen readers only announce when items collapse, not when they expand.
issuesFound.fix: Line in open(): After `item.open = true;` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Site search combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is assigned role="combobox" and aria-controls pointing to the suggestion listbox, but the _open and _close functions do not toggle aria-expanded to indicate whether the suggestion list is visible. This violates the WAI-ARIA combobox pattern requirements for expandable listboxes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in _open(): After `_searchState.open = true;` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line in _close(): After `_searchState.open = false;` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly, quarterly, annual) indicate the selected period visually but do not set aria-pressed to convey the active state to screen reader users. The buttons function as a single-select toggle group but lack the required pressed state semantics.
issuesFound.fix: Line in _syncToggleState(): Inside the forEach loop, after `const isActive = ...`, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: Form validation not setting aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds an error message element and links it to the field via aria-describedby, but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field itself. Screen readers cannot programmatically identify which fields contain errors when validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: Line in markInvalid(): After `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation not clearing aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error description reference from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute from the field. This causes screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid after it has been corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line in markValid(): After the describedby cleanup logic, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the code removes CSS classes and sets the hidden attribute on the overlay after a delay, but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element itself. This can leave the drawer content accessible to screen readers while it is visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line in close(): After `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown panels are closed, the code sets the hidden attribute after a CSS transition timeout, but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the panel element. This can leave the panel content in the accessibility tree while it is visually hidden, causing confusion for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line in _closeInstance(): After `inst.open = false;` add `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and in _openInstance after `inst.open = true;` add `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price values update visually with animation, but the new prices and period change are not announced to screen reader users via a live region, leaving them unaware of the update.
issuesFound.fix: Line in setPeriod(): After updating all plan cards, add `liveRegion.announce(\`Prices switched to \${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing\`);`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation missing focus trap and focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer declares a _trap variable but never initializes it with createFocusTrap. When opened, focus is not trapped within the drawer, and when closed, focus is not returned to the trigger button. Keyboard users can tab out of the modal drawer and lose their place in the document.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in init(): After `_initialised = true;` add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });`
  Line in open(): After `_lockScroll();` add `_trap.activate();`
  Line in close(): Ensure `_trap.deactivate();` is called (already present but will now function correctly).

issuesFound.title: Contact form first name field missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form first name input field (#contact-first-name) collects personal information but lacks the autocomplete="given-name" attribute. This prevents browsers from autofill functionality and reduces accessibility for users with cognitive disabilities who rely on autocomplete.
issuesFound.fix: Line in init(): After getting the first name field via `qs(SELECTORS.contactFirstName, _formEl)`, add `if (field) setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'given-name');`

issuesFound.title: Contact form last name field missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The last name input field (#contact-last-name) lacks the autocomplete="family-name" attribute, preventing proper autofill functionality and browser assistance for form completion.
issuesFound.fix: Line in init(): After getting the last name field, add `if (field) setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'family-name');`

issuesFound.title: Contact form email field missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The email input field (#contact-email) lacks the autocomplete="email" attribute, which is required for WCAG 2.2 Level AA compliance for forms collecting user data.
issuesFound.fix: Line in init(): After getting the email field, add `if (field) setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input field (#newsletter-email) lacks the autocomplete="email" attribute, preventing browsers from offering autofill suggestions for email addresses.
issuesFound.fix: Line in init(): After `_emailEl = qs(cfg.inputSelector);` add `if (_emailEl) setAttr(_emailEl, 'autocomplete', 'email');`

fpTitles: Dropdown menu panels not removed from accessibility tree when closed

Contact form last name field missing autocomplete attribute

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus are opened or closed, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never updated. The function manages the panel's hidden attribute and CSS classes but omits the required ARIA state on the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1878: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); Line 1890: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid

issuesFound.title: mobileNav aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the module initialises, even though the drawer starts in a closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls

issuesFound.fix: Line 1851: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, they are not initialised with aria-expanded="false", even though their panels start hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1939: Add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The search input combobox is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads, even though the suggestion list starts closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2744: Add setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile nav closes, the drawer element has CSS classes removed but is not explicitly hidden from assistive technologies with aria-hidden="true". While CSS may hide it, the accessibility tree update is not guaranteed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1787: Add setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true'); Line 1769 (open): Add removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel is hidden via the hidden attribute after a timeout, but aria-hidden="true" is not set explicitly when it closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1893: Add setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true'); Line 1878 (_openInstance): Add removeAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch suggestions not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion listbox is hidden using the hidden attribute, but aria-hidden is not set to true when closing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2616: Add setAttr(_listEl, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true'); Line 2609 (show): Add removeAttr(_listEl, ARIA.HIDDEN);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion panels not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ answer panels are hidden with the hidden attribute after animation, but aria-hidden="true" is not set explicitly when collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3423: Add setAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true'); Line 3400 (open): Add removeAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN);

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, the prices update visually but this change is not announced via a live region. Screen reader users are unaware that the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3068: Add liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll-to-top button is activated, the page scrolls to the top and focus is moved to the skip link, but no announcement is made confirming the action. While focus movement helps, an explicit announcement improves clarity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2160: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open missing focus trap activation and focus move
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus is not moved to the drawer, and the focus trap (created in init) is never activated. Keyboard users can tab outside the drawer while it is open, and focus is lost.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1776: Add if (_trap) _trap.activate(); and add _drawer.focus(); (or first focusable child) after activation.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing focus return to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. The deactivate() call on the focus trap would normally handle this, but since the trap is never activated (Issue 17), focus management is completely broken.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1796: Add _trigger.focus(); after _unlockScroll();

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu close missing focus return to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is closed (particularly via Escape key), focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. The returnFocus parameter exists in the function signature but is never used to manage focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1896: Add if (returnFocus) inst.trigger.focus(); before emitting the event.

fpTitles: mobileNav close missing focus return to trigger
dropdownMenu close missing focus return to trigger

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button in the NavDropdown component uses aria-controls and aria-haspopup but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state (isOpen) to assistive technology. Screen reader users cannot tell whether the submenu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1050: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-haspopup="true" className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger buttons inside MobileNav (className "mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger") control the visibility of submenus using the isExpanded state but do not expose this state via aria-expanded. Assistive technology users cannot perceive which sections are expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton in ThemeToggle renders only an emoji icon without an aria-label prop (despite the interface requiring it). Screen readers will announce the emoji character literally or as "button" without context, leaving users unaware of the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1200: <IconButton aria-label={`Switch to ${NEXT[theme]} theme (currently ${theme})`} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used to close the mobile navigation panel contains only a close character (✕) without an aria-label. The IconButton interface requires an aria-label prop, and without it, assistive technology cannot identify this as a "Close navigation" control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1100: <IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton for navigating to the previous testimonial contains only a chevron character (‹) and lacks an aria-label. Screen reader users hear only "button" or the literal character, with no indication that it navigates to the previous slide.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1750: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing toggle button visually indicates selection but lacks aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to assistive technology. Users cannot perceive which billing period is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1560: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: HeroSection video toggle missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button for the background video toggles the isPlaying state but only uses aria-label (which changes text). It lacks aria-pressed to indicate the current pressed/toggled state, which is the standard pattern for toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1250: <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" aria-pressed={isPlaying} aria-label={isPlaying ? 'Pause background video' : 'Play background video'} onClick={handleTogglePlay} className="hero-section__video-toggle">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: When a navigation item represents the current page (item.current === true), the link lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify which page they are currently on within the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1080: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: Section component labelledby prop ignored
issuesFound.explanation: The Section component accepts a labelledby prop but never applies it to the rendered element. When consumers pass aria-labelledby via this prop, the landmark remains unlabelled, breaking the accessible name for that region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 900: <Tag id={id} aria-labelledby={labelledby} className={['page-section', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar visual clone missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The second unordered list in IntegrationsBar is a visual clone for seamless scrolling animation but lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen readers will announce the integration partners twice, creating redundant verbosity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1300: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div used as interactive element without role
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation backdrop is a div with an onClick handler to close the menu, but it lacks role="button", tabindex="0", and keyboard event handlers. Keyboard-only users cannot activate this control to dismiss the modal.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1120: <div role="button" aria-label="Close navigation" tabIndex={0} onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }} className="mobile-nav-backdrop" />

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icon not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron indicator (▲/▼) inside the dropdown trigger is decorative but not marked with aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may announce the triangle character as "black up-pointing triangle" or similar, creating noise.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1060: {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.title: StarRating individual stars not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The StarRating component renders individual star characters (★/☆) inside spans that are not aria-hidden. While the container has an aria-label, some screen readers may still read the literal star characters, causing duplicate or confusing announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 850: {filled ? '★' : '☆'}

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy when loading
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading prop to show a spinner, but it does not set aria-busy="true" on the button element. Assistive technology users are not notified that the button is in a loading state and the action is in progress.
issuesFound.fix: Line 800: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav component renders a <nav> element without an aria-label. Since multiple nav elements exist on the page (SkipLinks, MobileNav, Footer), this landmark cannot be distinguished by screen reader users when navigating by region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1070: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component contains a <nav> element wrapping skip navigation links, but it lacks an aria-label. With multiple navigation landmarks on the page, users cannot distinguish this as the "Skip navigation" region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 650: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm ErrorMessage incorrect props
issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component expects a message prop, but NewsletterForm passes children and role instead. The error message will not render, and server-side form errors will be invisible to assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1800: <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-status" message="Something went wrong. Please try again in a moment." />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm ErrorMessage incorrect props
issuesFound.explanation: ContactForm passes children and role="alert" to ErrorMessage, which only accepts id and message props. The error text will not appear, and submission failures will not be announced to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1900: <ErrorMessage id="contact-server-error" message="Submission failed. Please check your connection and try again." />

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required asterisk not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk (*) indicating a required field is not marked with aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may announce "star" separately from the "(required)" text provided by VisuallyHidden, creating redundant or confusing output.
issuesFound.fix: Line 930: *

Line: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
